scope: Calculate pressure drop in bar or PSI across any pipe run based on flow rate, diameter, and material.

decision_context.system_boundary: Pipe Pressure Drop Calculator for small workshop or field-service decision support.
decision_context.single_operation_scope: Calculate pressure drop in bar or PSI across any pipe run based on flow rate, diameter, and material.
decision_context.decision_owner: shop owner, technician, estimator, field-service operator, or checker
decision_context.decision_after_output: Estimated pressure loss across the pipe run using user-specified friction context.
decision_context.cost_of_wrong_decision: Wrong inputs or misunderstood output can cause underpricing, cash leakage, avoidable rework, equipment downtime, or customer-margin loss.
decision_context.failure_mode_if_formula_wrong: Incorrect source values or formula binding can understate cost, capacity, downtime, scrap, or commercial exposure.
field_use_limitations: Decision support only. Verify high-risk work through qualified review before commitment.

calculation_basis.public_summary: The public result is based on normalized user inputs, validation blockers, evidence confidence, sensitivity ranking, and public-safe output redaction.

inputs.name: Flow Rate

physical_hard_bounds.semantic_error_message_min: Flow Rate cannot be below the lower physical or process bound.
physical_hard_bounds.semantic_error_message_max: No universal upper bound is enforced; review source evidence.

engineering_range.source: User-verified job, asset, or shop context. Advisory range only.

reference_values.source: Measured job record, quotation worksheet, supplier invoice, or owner-verified shop data.
reference_values.public_note: Reference values are advisory and must not auto-fill critical inputs.

evidence_requirement.public_help_text: Use measured or source-backed values where available. Estimated critical values reduce confidence.

inputs.user_help_text: Enter the verified flow rate for this job context.
inputs.warning_if_missing_or_estimated: Flow Rate is a decision-sensitive input. Missing or estimated values move the result to review.

rules.condition_description: Input violates physical or process hard bounds.
rules.semantic_message: Correct physically impossible or process-invalid inputs before calculation.
rules.why_it_matters: Invalid source values can create unsafe or commercially misleading outputs.
rules.suggested_action: Verify measurement, unit selection, and source evidence.

rules.condition_description: Critical input evidence is missing or estimated.
rules.semantic_message: Critical input evidence is missing. Treat the result as review-only.
rules.why_it_matters: Evidence quality can flip the decision.
rules.suggested_action: Attach source record or mark the value as verified.

rules.semantic_message: Non-finite numeric values are not allowed.
rules.why_it_matters: NaN or infinite outputs cannot support audit-ready decisions.
rules.suggested_action: Correct the input values and units.

rules.condition_description: Selected unit is missing or unsupported by conversion registry.
rules.semantic_message: Select a supported unit for every required input.
rules.why_it_matters: Formula execution requires normalized base-unit values.
rules.suggested_action: Choose a supported display unit or update conversion registry.

rules.condition_description: Reference values are used as defaults or decision authority.
rules.semantic_message: Reference values are advisory and cannot auto-fill critical inputs.

rules.name: Evidence confidence derating
rules.public_note: Low evidence confidence moves the result to REVIEW instead of silently accepting the output.

formulas.name: Pipe Pressure Drop Calculator server-side deterministic formula graph
assumptions: Inputs are user-verified shop, job, asset, or supplier values.
The server formula module owns exact deterministic calculation logic.
Public outputs are decision-support estimates, not certification or legal proof.
limitations: This tool does not replace qualified engineering review for safety-critical work.
Reference ranges are advisory and never override measured source data.
Exact formula expression is restricted to server-side execution files.
edge_cases: Missing evidence moves the result to REVIEW.
Non-finite or physically impossible inputs block execution.
Low denominator or zero-capacity conditions are handled fail-closed.
formulas.public_proof_summary: The result is derived from user-entered technical and commercial inputs after unit normalization, bound checks, evidence review, uncertainty screening, and public-safe redaction.
formulas.protected_methodology_summary: Server-only deterministic logic evaluates the job-specific demand, capacity, cost, margin, exposure, or loss metric without exposing exact formula expressions in public outputs.
formulas.checker_note: Review normalized inputs, evidence status, output sensitivity, and audit seal before making a commitment.
formulas.acceptance_logic: Proceed only when primary output, evidence confidence, and sensitivity review support the decision.
formulas.rejection_logic: Reject or reprice when the primary output indicates negative margin, capacity shortage, excessive exposure, or unsafe technical condition.
formulas.review_required_logic: Review is required when evidence is missing, uncertainty crosses the decision threshold, or a critical input is estimated.
formulas.uncertainty_expression: Server-side uncertainty model ranks input sensitivity and flags decision-threshold crossings without exposing exact formula expressions.

outputs.name: Flow Velocity
outputs.acceptance_threshold: Tool-specific review thresholds are enforced server-side when source data is sufficient.
outputs.review_threshold: Review is required when evidence is missing, confidence is low, or uncertainty crosses the decision threshold.
outputs.failure_threshold: Block only on impossible input, non-finite output, or unsafe contract violation.
outputs.public_explanation: Use this output to decide whether the job economics or technical setup should proceed, be repriced, or be reviewed.
outputs.operator_explanation: Use this output to decide whether the job economics or technical setup should proceed, be repriced, or be reviewed.
outputs.engineer_explanation: Review source inputs, unit normalization, and dominant sensitivity before committing the job.
outputs.owner_cfo_explanation: Use the result to decide whether the job, purchase, quote, or schedule protects cash and margin.
outputs.checker_explanation: Check source evidence, normalized units, uncertainty, and public-safe audit seal before relying on the result.
outputs.hidden_risk_note: Low-quality source inputs can flip the decision even when the numeric output appears acceptable.
outputs.suggested_action: Proceed only if the result remains acceptable after evidence review and sensitivity checks.

fields.primary_reason: Decision status is generated server-side from primary output, evidence confidence, validation blockers, uncertainty, and sensitivity.

user_profile_summary.operator: Use the output to decide whether the job can proceed or needs review.
user_profile_summary.engineer: Check normalized inputs, sensitivity, and limitations before relying on the result.
user_profile_summary.owner_cfo: Use the cost or exposure metric to protect cash, margin, and schedule commitment.
user_profile_summary.checker_auditor: Verify evidence, audit seal, redaction status, and public proof pack.

hidden_risk_explanations: Estimated or missing critical inputs can flip the result.
Supplier, material, or site conditions may require manual review.
next_best_actions: Proceed, reprice, reject, or escalate according to status.

business_impact_contract.public_value_statement: Translate the raw technical result into a shop-floor decision signal for cost, margin, waste, downtime, capacity, or rework exposure.

states.label: REVIEW
states.technical_message: Review required due to evidence, range, or sensitivity condition.

states.label: WARNING
states.technical_message: Result is close to a commercial or technical decision threshold.

states.label: CRITICAL
states.technical_message: High exposure or unsafe condition requires escalation.

proof_pack.purpose: Generate review-ready engineering evidence for technical checking, internal review, audit discussion, and shop-floor documentation.
public_report_rules: Do not expose exact formula expressions.
Do not reproduce restricted standard tables.
Do not mention third-party engineering software brands.
Show source context, use scope, limitation, evidence status, protected methodology, result interpretation, uncertainty, and audit seal.

audit_trail_contract.tamper_warning: Changing any input, unit, schema version, formula version, or output invalidates the audit seal.

input_groups.title: Commercial Context
input_groups.description: Cost, rate, price, margin, and exposure inputs.

input_groups.title: Evidence and Review
input_groups.description: Source quality and review status controls.

test_plan: schema generation prompt not used in runtime
normalized preview does not execute formulas
reference values are visible but do not autofill critical inputs
FMEA trigger at high utilization or threshold risk
state machine is bound to UniversalIndustrialDecisionForm

metadata.change_log_summary: Initial Free Tool V5.3.1 schema and server-only formula module generated from user-provided registry.
